title: Ecological Report
text: Ecological Report main interpretive output camtrapReport. assembles processed camera-trap data article-style document combines narrative text, tables, figures, maps. tutorial explains report organised, section designed show, main outputs can interpreted. practical introduction input data preparation first use, see Get started page.

what: How the Ecological Report is organised
title: Ecological Report
text: Ecological Report structured standard scientific-style document. typically includes introduction, methods, results, acknowledgements, appendix, references. Within sections, camtrapReport inserts outputs generated selected analysis modules. exact contents report depend modules included workflow. sections provide study context methodological information, whereas others present ecological summaries, figures, maps.

title: Ecological Report
text: Introduction provides ecological monitoring context report. briefly explains study conducted, taxa groups monitored, ecological questions report intended address.

title: Ecological Report
text: Methods section explains data collected processed. describes study area, camera deployment, sampling effort, image handling, main steps used prepare data analysis.

title: Ecological Report
text: Results section contains main ecological outputs generated selected modules. Depending report settings, section may include summaries captures, temporal activity patterns, species richness, spatial density, habitat use, species co-occurrence, species accumulation, abundance-related trends.

title: Ecological Report
text: section records package attribution, project support, acknowledgements linked report.

title: Ecological Report
text: Appendix can include supplementary material, optional image outputs additional supporting content.

title: Ecological Report
text: References section lists main literature sources cited report text methods.

title: Ecological Report
text: Study Area section introduces site provides spatial context report. usually includes location study site, approximate extent, short description monitoring area. section helps readers understand survey took place moving analytical outputs.

title: Ecological Report
text: Camera Locations section shows camera traps deployed. current report format, presented interactive map camera positions displayed survey year full monitoring period. output helps users: - inspect spatial distribution deployments, - compare yearly coverage, - review camera metadata linked map points, - understand habitat spatial coverage may influence later results.

title: Ecological Report
text: Sampling effort describes much survey effort invested across monitoring period. report, section summarises number active cameras, deployment duration, total effort across years. section important many ecological outputs depend effort. Differences capture numbers species detections interpreted context much sampling conducted.

title: Ecological Report
text: Image Processing section explains images classified, grouped, validated, archived. provides context raw media transformed analysis-ready observations. section helps users understand data provenance behind report outputs.

title: Ecological Report
text: Data Processing section explains input data cleaned, harmonised, summarised, prepared ecological analysis. links original camera-trap data final report outputs. section especially useful documenting reproducibility making analytical workflow transparent.

title: Ecological Report
text: Captures section provides overview observed taxa summarises key capture-based metrics. example report, includes table frequently detected species values number captures, relative abundance index, number capture locations, total number photos. section gives readers first summary species dominate dataset frequently recorded.

title: Ecological Report
text: Activity Patterns section describes detections distributed across 24-hour day. output typically shown smoothed activity curve species, often confidence intervals sunrise/sunset reference lines. outputs help users interpret whether species mainly diurnal, nocturnal, crepuscular, can also reveal differences among species daily timing activity.

title: Ecological Report
text: Richness section summarises many species detected across camera locations. current report style, richness visualised interactive map point size colour reflect number species recorded location. section helps identify biodiversity hotspots, uneven sampling outcomes, variation local species diversity across study area.

title: Ecological Report
text: Species Co-occurrence section summarises pairwise associations among species recorded across camera locations. current report, shown correlation-style matrix. outputs help users explore whether species tend recorded together apart across sites. useful descriptive comparison, interpreted cautiously co-occurrence imply ecological interaction.

title: Ecological Report
text: Spatial Density section visualises spatial concentration detections across study area. current report format, presented interactive density surface overlaid map. module helps users identify areas relatively high low detection intensity compare spatial patterns among species across survey years.

title: Ecological Report
text: Habitat Preferences section compares capture-rate distributions across habitat types. current example, output shown stacked bar chart summarising relative contribution habitat type species. section helps users assess broad differences habitat association provides accessible summary detections distributed across available habitat categories.

title: Ecological Report
text: Species accumulation curves show recorded richness changes increasing sampling effort. report, curves presented survey year comparison view. outputs help users assess whether survey captured detectable species pool whether additional sampling likely reveal species.

title: Ecological Report
text: section summarises temporal trends species records across survey years. Depending selected output, may include number captures, capture rate, number locations, REM-based density estimates. outputs useful comparing change time, always interpreted sampling effort detection conditions mind.

title: Ecological Report
text: useful way read Ecological Report move context interpretation: Read Introduction Methods understand study design. Review Camera Locations Sampling Effort assess survey coverage. Use Captures first summary observed species. Interpret Activity Patterns, Richness, Co-occurrence, Spatial Density, Habitat Preferences, Accumulation Curves complementary views dataset. Read abundance-related trends context effort species detectability. way, report functions collection separate figures, connected set outputs support ecological interpretation.

title: Ecological Report
text: Ecological Report modular. Users can include selected sections, omit others, adapt structure final report match monitoring objective. means report can used standard reporting format flexible framework customised ecological summaries.

title: Ecological Report
text: Ecological Report designed turn processed camera-trap data structured, interpretable, reproducible document. article-style format combines context, methods, ecological outputs, narrative explanation single report. understanding purpose module outputs relate one another, users can read report effectively adapt easily different study goals.

title: Package Overview
text: camtrapReport designed help users move raw camera-trap records clear, reproducible ecological outputs. Rather treating data cleaning, summarisation, analysis, visualisation, reporting separate tasks, package brings together single workflow. makes easier produce consistent outputs across study sites survey periods reducing manual effort required prepare reports. vignette provides conceptual overview package organised. explains main parts workflow, role central camReport object, data checks, analyses, report sections fit together. practical introduction installation, input data, first use, see Get started page.

what: What camtrapReport is designed to do
title: Package Overview
text: package two main aims. First, helps users assess whether camera-trap dataset complete, consistent, ready analysis. Second, supports generation standardised ecological reports combine summaries, figures, maps, narrative text reproducible format. practice, camtrapReport can used : organise prepare camera-trap data downstream analysis, identify common data-quality problems reporting, automate ecological analyses, generate reports assemble outputs article-style document can reviewed, shared, reproduced later. package especially useful type reporting must repeated across projects, sites, years, species groups.

title: Package Overview
text: centre package camReport object, created camData(). object acts main workflow container. stores imported dataset, user settings, intermediate results, outputs generated different parts workflow. Keeping elements together one object helps package remain organised reproducible. Instead passing many separate files arguments functions, users work single object keeps data, settings, outputs connected.

title: Package Overview
text: camtrapReport built modular system. means many parts ecological report handled separate modules, responsible specific output analysis. module may produce table, figure, map, summary statistic, block narrative text. modular structure makes package flexible. Users can run parts need, combine selected outputs customised report, extend package adding new modules needed.

title: Package Overview
text: useful way think package workflow three connected layers: Data preparation, input data read, checked, cleaned, organised; Analysis modules, specific summaries ecological outputs generated; Report sections, outputs assembled readable final document. Modules supply content, report sections provide structure. sections built modular outputs, can included, omitted, reordered, expanded depending aims report.

what: Camtrap-DP as the standard input format
title: Package Overview

Using standard format important improves comparability makes easier generate kinds outputs across multiple datasets.

title: camtrapReport
text: camtrapReport helps turn camera-trap data standardised, reproducible ecological outputs workflow designed automate much reporting process possible. package combines data quality checks, summarisation, visualisation, ecological analysis modular framework, helping users move efficiently raw records interpretable results. automated workflow improves comparability, scalability, reproducibility across sites time, reducing technical barriers supporting transparent, efficient, timely ecological reporting. main functions package include: Data status report, performs automated diagnostics data quality completeness across key components camera-trap datasets, including spatial temporal information, key column availability, annotations, validation status, observation types capture method. checks help users identify missing information, inconsistencies, unusual values may require correction completion. Ecological report, produces standardised outputs modular components. Reports can include descriptive information, summaries key data, visualisations important patterns, ecological assessments analyses.

title: camtrapReport
text: camtrapReport designed datasets provided , converted , Camtrap-DP standard format, community-developed data exchange format camera-trap data. Several camera-trap data management systems support Camtrap-DP export format, including Agouti TRAPPER. also possible convert data camera-trap sources, including manually managed datasets Wildlife Insights exports, Camtrap-DP format use camtrapReport.

title: camtrapReport
text: Three example datasets provided . open-access camera-trap datasets, available GBIF, follow Camtrap-DP standard can used test camtrapReport. Note: habitat data study area files prepared exclusively testing camtrapReport represent official data products published original dataset owners.

title: camtrapReport
text: Install camtrapReport standard remotes workflow: Note: camtrapReport requires R ≥ 4.1.0. unsure current R version, run R.version.string R console.
code: # install using the "remotes" package if (!requireNamespace("remotes", quietly = TRUE)) {   install.packages("remotes") } remotes::install_github("spatialecology/camtrapReport")

title: camtrapReport
text: addition loading camtrapReport package, depending report sections methods use, additional packages may required. ensure full functionality, run helper function, install_All, installs package dependencies: Note: may prompted approve package installations compile packages source. Make sure active internet connection.
code: library(camtrapReport) install_All()

title: camtrapReport
text: remotes::install_github() fails, common cause missing GitHub personal access token (PAT). set one , run: installation still fails, please open issue include full error message, output sessionInfo(), information whether error occurred install_github() install_All().
code: install.packages(c("remotes", "usethis", "gitcreds")) usethis::create_github_token()  # Opens GitHub in your browser — create and copy the token gitcreds::gitcreds_set()        # Paste the token when prompted in R remotes::install_github("spatialecology/camtrapReport")

code: tx <- reportSection(name='introduction',title='Introduction',parent=NULL,txt="This is introduction section...")  tx #> class                                 : .textSection  #> ===========================================================  #> name of the object                    :  introduction  #> title                                 :  Introduction  #> parent                                :   #> is R code included?                   :  FALSE  #> -----------------------------------------------------------       # in the following text section, we add an R chunk code which gets access to the camReport object # through using the object keyword:  tx2 <- reportSection(name='method',title='Methods',parent=NULL,txt="Here, we show the usage of ...",                       code_setting={c(echo=FALSE,results="asis",warn=FALSE)},                       packages=c("gt","dplyr"),                       code = {                                                  object$camera_setup |>                         gt() |>                         tab_header(                         title = md("**Camera Deployment Summary**"),                         subtitle = md("**Table 1.** Details of camera deployments per year")) |>                         cols_label(                         year = "Year",                         number_camtraps = "Camera Traps",                         deployment_period = "Deployment Period",                         setup_period = "Setup Period",                         pickup_period = "Pickup Period") |>                         tab_options(                         table.font.size = px(14),                         heading.title.font.size = px(16),                         heading.subtitle.font.size = px(12),                         row.striping.include_table_body = TRUE) |>                         opt_row_striping() |>                         tab_style(style = list(                         cell_borders(sides = "bottom", color = "gray", weight = px(1))),                         locations = cells_body()) |>                         tab_style(                         style = list(cell_text(weight = "bold")),locations = cells_column_labels())                                                                         }                       )                        tx2 #> class                                 : title: Getting help with camtrapReport
text: Use GitHub issue tracker : reproducible bugs broken outputs installation problems include error message Please include: camtrapReport version R version operating system minimal reproducible example sessionInfo() full error message usage questions, workflow advice, interpretation questions, please use Discussions package documentation instead opening bug report.
